name: Planet Labs - Dove - Planet Pelican Gen-1 Imager Sat sso_vnir_pelican-gen1_0

@comment: Sensor: TDI pushbroom, 0.5 m GSD, 8 km swath, f=4.03 m, 12288-px PAN
instrument.name: Planet Pelican Gen-1 Imager
@notes: Focal length 4.03 m, pixel pitch ~4.24 µm (back-calculated: GSD=0.5m at 475km → p=0.5*4.03/475e3).
F# = f/D → D = 4.03/11.5 ≈ 0.35 m aperture diameter (estimated).
FOV angleHeight set to 0.1° (single TDI line at any instant; scene collected along-track).
FOV angleWidth = 2*arctan(12288*4.24e-6 / (2*4.03)) ≈ 0.74° cross-track.
sceneFOV angleHeight = 10° represents a typical long along-track collect strip.
sceneFOV angleWidth = 0.97° matches the published 8 km product swath at 475 km.
Maneuver: Circular 30° diameter represents ±15° 3D agility at 15°/s slew rate.
6 MS bands: Blue(465–518nm), Green(547–585nm), Red(650–682nm), RE(699–716nm),
NIR-Wide(779–885nm), NIR-Narrow(846–887nm). PAN: 450–800nm.
operatingWavelength and bandwidth approximate PAN band center and width.

@notes: Instrument type: Passive Optical Scanner used as best available model for TIR pushbroom.

operatingWavelength: 10 µm (center of 8–12 µm LWIR band).
bandwidth: 4 µm (full 8–12 µm LWIR window).

maneuver: Single_Roll_Only ±30° models the published off-nadir pointing capability.
4 spectral bands within 8–12 µm (band centers not publicly disclosed).
Daytime AND night-time capable (passive thermal emission, no solar illumination required).

Onboard Nvidia Jetson GPU for real-time fire detection — fire alerts within 3 min of detection.

Day AND night capable (passive thermal emission sensing).

name: Planet Labs - Pelican - Planet Pelican Gen-1 Imager Sat mio_vnir_pelican-gen1_0

@comment: Sensor: TDI pushbroom, 0.5 m GSD, 8 km swath, f=4.03 m, 12288-px PAN
instrument.name: Planet Pelican Gen-1 Imager
@notes: Focal length 4.03 m, pixel pitch ~4.24 µm (back-calculated: GSD=0.5m at 475km → p=0.5*4.03/475e3).
F# = f/D → D = 4.03/11.5 ≈ 0.35 m aperture diameter (estimated).
FOV angleHeight set to 0.1° (single TDI line at any instant; scene collected along-track).
FOV angleWidth = 2*arctan(12288*4.24e-6 / (2*4.03)) ≈ 0.74° cross-track.
sceneFOV angleHeight = 10° represents a typical long along-track collect strip.
sceneFOV angleWidth = 0.97° matches the published 8 km product swath at 475 km.
Maneuver: Circular 30° diameter represents ±15° 3D agility at 15°/s slew rate.
6 MS bands: Blue(465–518nm), Green(547–585nm), Red(650–682nm), RE(699–716nm),
NIR-Wide(779–885nm), NIR-Narrow(846–887nm). PAN: 450–800nm.
operatingWavelength and bandwidth approximate PAN band center and width.

Steerable: Double_Roll_Only (left-look AND right-look, 15°–55° each side)
instrument.name: Capella X-SAR

mode.@notes: 1.2 m azimuth resolution, ~10–30 km swath

minimumPRF: 6333 Hz (2*vs/L = 2*7600/2.4).

maneuver: Double_Roll_Only models bilateral side-look (left and right of track),

peakTransmitPower: 2000 W (2 kW, consistent with ICEYE/Capella class).
systemNoiseFigure: 4 dB (typical X-band T/R module).
radarLosses: 3.5 dB (typical microwave path + feed losses).
atmosLoss: 0.5 dB (2-way, dry atmosphere at X-band).

name: CNES - SMASH - SMASH Ka-band Nadir Altimeter (BWI/CNES) Sat sso_alt_smash-ka-altimeter_0

@comment: 6. Radar Altimeter — CNES/LEGOS SMASH / Blue Water Intelligence Ka-band nadir
Ka-band nadir pulse-limited, 35 GHz, 3200 MHz BW, 10 cm height accuracy
Not steerable — pure nadir altimeter (point measurement below satellite)
instrument.name: SMASH Ka-band Nadir Altimeter (BWI/CNES)
@notes: Model: Altimeter (Aslan15 fork AltimeterSensorModel — extends BasicSensorModel with
chirpBandwidth and pulseWidth parameters).
Technique: nadir pulse-limited Ka-band radar altimeter. Frequency: ~35.75 GHz.
chirpBandwidth: 3200 MHz (published). Height resolution ≈ c/(2*B) = 3e8/(2*3.2e9) ≈ 4.7 cm.
pulseWidth: 1/(2*B) = 1/(2*3.2e9) ≈ 0.31 ns (compressed pulse width; uncompressed longer).
Height accuracy: 10 cm (end-to-end, including POD contribution).
Radial POD requirement: 5 cm (met via bi-frequency GNSS receiver, not modelled here).
Antenna: ~35 cm parabolic dish. Transmitted power: <0.5 W (efficient due to high water σ₀).
fieldOfViewGeometry diameter: 0.5° approximates the nadir beam footprint
(pulse-limited circle ~few km; angular equivalent at 500 km altitude ≈ 0.5°).
Coverage model: virtual stations at track × water-body intersections (~30,000 globally).
NOT a swath imager — coverage is 1D along ground tracks only.
Min river width detectable: 50 m. Min lake size: 100×100 m.
10-nanosatellite constellation on single SSO plane provides daily continental water coverage.
Day AND night, all-weather capable.
